findings.evidence: PR lines 53-57 prove divisibility and apply `Polynomial.natDegree_le_of_dvd`; Mathlib already has `mathlib/Mathlib/FieldTheory/Minpoly/Field.lean:41: theorem degree_le_of_ne_zero {p : A[X]} (pnz : p ≠ 0) (hp : Polynomial.aeval x p = 0) : degree (minpoly A x) ≤ degree p`.
findings.fix: Replace the `minpoly.dvd`/`Polynomial.natDegree_le_of_dvd` proof with `minpoly.degree_le_of_ne_zero F x hpoly_ne hroot`, converting the resulting degree bound to `natDegree ≤ 2`.
findings.issue: `minpoly_natDegree_le_two_of_sq_mem` reproves the general Mathlib fact that a nonzero annihilating polynomial bounds the minimal polynomial degree.

summary: The refactor mostly uses Mathlib's power-basis API, but the new minimal-polynomial degree helper still reimplements an existing Mathlib bound.
